api/compiler.title: ToolCompiler
api/compiler.description: ToolCompiler runs the Parse → Embed → Overload → Link pipeline and emits a compiled artifact.

api/dispatch.title: Dispatch API
api/dispatch.description: The low-level dispatch API. These are the functions that ToolRuntime calls internally. You can use them directly if you want to manage DispatchContext yourself.

api/mcp-server.title: MCPServer
api/mcp-server.description: MCPServer provides a MCP 2025-11-25 compliant HTTP server. It handles JSON-RPC requests, Server-Sent Events, and the MCP discovery endpoint.

api/runtime.title: ToolRuntime
api/runtime.description: ToolRuntime is the top-level class. It owns the DispatchContext, manages the lifecycle of ToolClass objects, and provides the public dispatch API.

architecture.title: Architecture
architecture.description: "The big idea is messaging." — Alan Kay

cli/compile.title: compile
cli/compile.description: Reads tool manifests from a source directory, generates semantic embeddings, groups tools into dispatch classes, and emits a compiled artifact.

cli/index.title: CLI Reference
cli/index.description: The @smallchat/core package ships a CLI for compiling manifests, inspecting artifacts, testing dispatch resolution, and running the MCP server.

cli/inspect.title: inspect
cli/inspect.description: Inspects a compiled artifact and prints structured information about its contents.

cli/resolve.title: resolve
cli/resolve.description: Tests dispatch resolution against a compiled artifact. Given a natural-language intent, prints the matched tool and confidence score. Useful for verifying that your tool descriptions produce the dispatch behaviour you expect.

cli/serve.title: serve
cli/serve.description: Starts a MCP 2025-11-25 compliant HTTP server that exposes your compiled tools to any MCP-aware client.

concepts/dispatch.title: Dispatch
concepts/dispatch.description: The dispatch layer is the hot path. It takes a natural-language intent, resolves it to a concrete implementation, and invokes it — either as a single call or as a streaming generator.

concepts/index.title: Deep Dive
concepts/index.description: This section documents the internals of smallchat for engineers who want to understand or extend the runtime.

concepts/overloading.title: Function Overloading
concepts/overloading.description: A single ToolSelector can map to multiple implementations with different parameter signatures. The OverloadTable resolves which implementation to call based on argument types and arity — analogous to method overloading in statically-typed languages, but resolved at runtime.

concepts/resolution-cache.title: Resolution Cache
concepts/resolution-cache.description: The ResolutionCache is an LRU cache that stores resolved dispatches — analogous to the inline method cache in objc_msgSend. On a cache hit, dispatch skips the embedding and vector search entirely. On a cache miss, the full resolution runs and the result is stored for future calls.

concepts/sc-object.title: SCObject System
concepts/sc-object.description: The SCObject hierarchy is an NSObject-inspired base class for typed parameter passing. It enables runtime type checking, auto-wrapping of plain JavaScript values, and a consistent object model across dispatch boundaries.

concepts/selector-table.title: Selector Table
concepts/selector-table.description: The SelectorTable is the semantic interning layer — analogous to sel_registerName in the Objective-C runtime. It ensures that semantically equivalent intent strings resolve to the same canonical ToolSelector, deduplicated by vector similarity.

concepts/streaming.title: Streaming
concepts/streaming.description: smallchat opens the actual provider stream. Dispatch resolves the intent once, then hands control straight to the LLM provider. Tokens arrive the moment they are generated. No waiting for the full result.

concepts/swizzling.title: Method Swizzling
concepts/swizzling.description: Method swizzling replaces a tool implementation at runtime — analogous to method swizzling in Objective-C. The original implementation can be preserved and optionally called from the replacement.

concepts/tool-class.title: ToolClass & ToolProxy
concepts/tool-class.description: ToolClass is the provider abstraction — analogous to a class in Objective-C. It groups related tools under a single dispatch table, supports superclass chains for inheritance-based fallback, and declares protocol conformance.

getting-started.title: Getting Started
getting-started.description: Get from zero to a running dispatch in under five minutes.

intro.title: Introduction
intro.description: "The big idea is messaging." — Alan Kay

manifests/examples.title: Example Manifests
manifests/examples.description: The examples/ directory in the smallchat repository contains ready-to-use manifests for common providers. Below are four representative examples.

manifests/format.title: Manifest Format
manifests/format.description: A provider manifest is a JSON file that describes a tool provider and its tools. Manifests are the input to the compile command and the ToolCompiler API.

what-it-does.title: What it does
what-it-does.description: smallchat is a message-passing tool compiler for LLM-powered applications. It solves one specific problem: given a natural-language intent from an LLM, find and invoke the best-matching tool — reliably, fast, and without hallucination.
